Composition.Programmer 1: Fix and enhance main.py backend Flask application for EventPlanning web application. Specifically, ensure all route functions correctly handle data and edge cases, and maintain the existing functionality of reading and writing data from local text files in the 'data' directory. Coordinate with Programmer 2 to ensure backend routes correspond exactly to frontend template expectations and navigation URLs.
Composition.Programmer 2: Implement all required HTML template files for the EventPlanning web application according to the detailed page design specifications in the requirements document. This includes creating the following templates with the specified elements and IDs  dashboard.html, events.html, event_details.html, ticket_booking.html, participants.html, venues.html, venue_details.html, schedules.html, and bookings.html. Ensure templates are placed in the Flask app's 'templates' directory and include all required UI elements with correct IDs for seamless integration with the backend routes. Coordinate with Programmer 1 to confirm that template rendering matches backend data and route logic.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
